---
title: ""
---

<div class="about-hero reveal">
<h1 class="gradient-text-animated">About Markuss</h1>
<p class="about-lead">I build analytics systems that hold up after the presentation ends: measured, testable, and tied to a real operating decision.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-grid">

<div class="about-section reveal">
<h2>Background</h2>
<p>I am a Business Analytics student at BYU-Idaho with a 4.0 GPA and minors in Statistics and Data Science. I came into analytics through real operating work: campaign performance, sponsorship prioritization, public-health communication, enterprise web measurement, and systems where a weak metric can lead a team in the wrong direction.</p>
<p>That is why I rarely start with the dashboard. I start with the decision, the source data, the failure modes, and the threshold that should change what someone does next.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-section reveal">
<h2>Current Work</h2>
<p>At Volvo Group, I work on enterprise digital analytics across Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, forecasting, machine learning, funnel monitoring, and web measurement. The work covers digital journeys serving more than 3 million monthly users.</p>
<p>I have led martech rationalization that removed two software dependencies and reduced annual run-rate by $50,000. I also built statistical monitoring that surfaced a drop in high-intent funnel submissions from 9% to 5%, making the issue visible early enough for the team to act.</p>
</div>

</div>

<div class="about-proof reveal-stagger">
<div><strong>3M+</strong><span>monthly users covered by enterprise measurement</span></div>
<div><strong>$50K</strong><span>annual software run-rate eliminated</span></div>
<div><strong>2,250</strong><span>qualified applicants generated</span></div>
<div><strong>$370K</strong><span>sponsorship value surfaced</span></div>
<div><strong>175K</strong><span>people reached in public health</span></div>
<div><strong>6.5B+</strong><span>Roblox place visits across shipped work</span></div>
</div>

<div class="about-grid">

<div class="about-section reveal">
<h2>How I Think</h2>
<div class="about-values">
<div class="value-item"><div class="value-icon">01</div><div><strong>Find the constraint</strong><p>The useful problem is usually the bottleneck limiting throughput, conversion, access, quality, or trust.</p></div></div>
<div class="value-item"><div class="value-icon">02</div><div><strong>Make failure visible</strong><p>Monitoring, provenance, audit trails, and explicit caveats matter as much as the successful output.</p></div></div>
<div class="value-item"><div class="value-icon">03</div><div><strong>Test the claim</strong><p>A model result or management narrative should survive an honest attempt to disprove it.</p></div></div>
<div class="value-item"><div class="value-icon">04</div><div><strong>Ship the handoff</strong><p>The work is not finished until another person can inspect it, use it, and understand the decision it supports.</p></div></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="about-section reveal">
<h2>Where I Am Going</h2>
<p>I am targeting analytics engineering, product analytics, applied data science, and healthcare or financial data-product roles. I am most useful where the source data is messy, the operating environment is complex, and the answer needs more discipline than a one-off notebook.</p>
<p>Healthcare remains personal. Family health challenges shaped the kind of impact I want over the long term. I am also drawn to enterprise operations, finance, and large product systems because they teach the same skills: capacity, risk, measurement, governance, and decisions under constraint.</p>
</div>

</div>

<div class="about-tech reveal">
<h2><span class="gradient-text">Technical Range</span></h2>
<div class="tech-grid">
<div class="tech-card"><h4>Analytics Engineering</h4><p>SQL, ETL, data modeling, metric governance, source reconciliation, APIs, testing, and reporting datasets</p></div>
<div class="tech-card"><h4>Statistical Systems</h4><p>Regression, robustness testing, forecasting, simulation, anomaly detection, experiment design, and uncertainty</p></div>
<div class="tech-card"><h4>Applied Machine Learning</h4><p>scikit-learn, XGBoost, SHAP, model comparison, evaluation, monitoring, and human review workflows</p></div>
<div class="tech-card"><h4>Business Intelligence</h4><p>Power BI, DAX, Power Query, Adobe CJA, Excel modeling, KPI design, and executive reporting</p></div>
<div class="tech-card"><h4>Product Development</h4><p>Python, R, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Node.js, Next.js, TypeScript, Quarto, and package development</p></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="about-cta reveal">
<p>I am graduating in April 2027 and am interested in teams that expect analysts to own the problem, not just the chart.</p>
<a class="btn-primary" href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/markuss-saule/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Connect on LinkedIn</a>
</div>
